goal: Write a punchy ~130-word X/Twitter post that pitches 'verifiable agent loops': running a closed-loop agent inside an EigenCompute TEE so the whole loop is attestable and can custody funds. It must land the core insight that everyone in the agent-looping discourse is stuck on the eval/trust gate, and EigenCompute is the one thing that makes an unattended loop trustworthy. Confident, technical-but-readable, no hashtags, no emoji spam. Aim for roughly 120-160 words — prioritise landing the insight cleanly over hitting an exact word count.
rubric: PASS if ALL hold: (1) clearly explains what a closed agent loop is in one line; (2) names the trust/eval-gate problem that unattended loops have; (3) explains how a TEE (verifiable execution + dedicated wallet + audit trail) solves it; (4) reads like a sharp practitioner wrote it, not marketing fluff; (5) has a strong opening line and a clean closing line; (6) no hashtags, no emoji spam, no buzzword salad; (7) length is in a sensible range (~110-180 words). Judge on substance and craft — do NOT fail a strong post over small length differences. FAIL only if it is vague, over-hyped, buries the insight, or clearly far too long/short.
